entry.title: How to upload the SSL certificates from application server or presentation server into ABAP system.
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Good Morning.

We have requirement which related automate the Strustss02 functionality through APIs .

In the above requirement ,we need to download the certificates from specified portal and need to upload the certificates into the system.

Kindly let me know is there any BAPI are available for this functionality.

Thank you,

Naresh.V

entry.title: Inconsistencies occurred in target system when we transported Storage location addresses
entry.content:
Hi Team, 

Inconsistencies occurred in target system when we transported Storage location addresses from source system.

In the source system: The number of addresses in the TWLAD table matches the number in the ADRC table.

In the target system: The number of addresses in the TWLAD table does not match the number in the ADRC table.

 

SAP Product: S4HANA ON PREMISE

Release: 2023

 

Thanks and Regards,

Manohar.

entry.title: Re: What's New in the Newly Repackaged SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh
entry.content:
Hi Henry,

We don't support this seamless migration to the new plan yet. As of today, it will be a different subscription. But by end of this year, we plan to introduce a seamless switch to the new plan where the existing brokers will continue to run as is. Of course, this is still WIP and the timelines are tentative. 

Regards, Gautham

entry.title: Re: S4HANA Public Cloud_Order confirmation form_change ou...
entry.content:
Hello,

I would assume the order confirmation change message is not enabled. Please go to the SSCUI 101214 to enable change document output type for sales document.

Best regards,

Yong

entry.title: CDS View IFICOMPANYCODE not extracting all codes
entry.content:
Hi there

S4H Public Cloud CDS View I_COMPANYCODE shows all codes available. We are using IFICOMPANYCODE to extract the codes into BW4H. But we don't get all codes as shown in I_COMPANYCODE.

Any ideas?

Thanks!

entry.title: Cash pooling process in SAP Bydesign
entry.content:
I wanted to check if we can get more info on CASH POOLING Process in SAP Business Bydesign.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Cash pooling process in SAP Bydesign
entry.content:
Cash Pooling process is not available in SAP Business Bydesign.

Currently considered this is a new requirement. 

We request you to post your requirement in our Influence sap :  https://influence.sap.com/

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Application: Issue in OData POST/PUT Operation failed with CSRF-Token validation fail
entry.content: Hello @Venkat,

entry.title: Unleash your procurement transformation potential with SAP Signavio for SAP Ariba
entry.content:
Part I: Overview of SAP Signavio capabilities

In today's dynamic business landscape, the procurement function stands at the crossroads of driving operational efficiency, reducing costs, and enhancing agility. The challenges are manifold, encompassing a wide range from maverick buying and late deliveries to manual interventions and a lack of process compliance.

One aspect needs to be clear: processes are at the center stage to address these challenges.

In this blog post series, we'll explore the capabilities offered by SAP Signavio to assist organizations in

  • realizing untapped value quickly,
  • harmonizing processes across the organization,
  • and building a resilient future in procurement.

 

I. Realize Untapped Value Quickly

The journey to realizing untapped value begins with identifying, analyzing and prioritizing improvement opportunities effectively.

Therefore, SAP Signavio provides pre-built analysis capabilities: 

With procurement process flows and associated blockers, organizations gain an "X-ray" view into their procurement process performance, allowing for swift identification of bottlenecks. Process performance indicators provide insights into the backlog and throughput of your SAP Ariba system.

With cross-system dashboards, as part of the plug and gain approach, customers can deep-dive into very specific issues to analyze and act on your most relevant improvement opportunities in Source to Pay. Internal benchmarking and filtering capabilities enable organizations to compare their performance across the organization and analyze current values and trends.

This, coupled with correction and innovation recommendations, empowers organizations to make quick fixes and improve procurement FTE productivity, days payable outstanding, and reducing data management costs as well as to make use of recommended SAP Ariba and Intelligent spend Management capabilities, ultimately realizing tangible value outcomes. 

 

PRODUCT HIGHLIGHTS

Procurement Process Flows and Process Performance Indicators      

  

  • Within hours, get pre-defined insights into processes and their adoption running in SAP Ariba Procurement, SAP Ariba Sourcing, SAP Ariba Contracts, and SAP Ariba Supplier Management
  • Leverage a fast track to process improvements, ranging from manual purchase order creation and maverick buying to late supplier payments
  • Find the root cause of your issue with advanced filtering capabilities, e.g., on material groups, purchasing organizations, or company codes
  • Gain visibility into buying and clearing behavior to increase efficiency, decrease obstacles, and better optimize available resources
  • Understand supplier performance to prioritize them according to the PO spend, their delays, and on-time delivery rates

 

SAP Signavio plug and gain approach: Improvement opportunity dashboards

  

  • Identify manual changes and reprocessing in purchase orders, and requisitions to improve operational efficiency and reduce costs of process execution
  • Improve your automation across SAP Ariba and SAP ERP
  • Increase your supplier delivery reliability, e.g., compare different suppliers of materials so you can identify the most suitable supplier for each material, or avoid shortages of materials
  • Improve your material and supplier master data quality to reduce errors, improve decision-making, and enhance overall operational efficiency
  • Improve your three-way match rate to avoid under or over deliveries which lead to issues in production, and warehouse logistics and increase storage risks

 

Correction and Innovation Recommendations

  • Quickly identify procurement process issues and get guidance on how to resolve them, such as adjusting invoice exception configuration and closing sourcing events with a pending status
  • Optimize data management and reduce data management costs e.g. cancelling requisition items and purchase orders no longer expected
  • Understand which additional capabilities could be leveraged to solve your Source to Pay issues more fundamentally, e.g., AI and ML solutions to increase automation rates.

 

II. Harmonize Processes Across Organizations

Achieving process harmonization across the organization is crucial for enhancing efficiency and driving alignment.

SAP Signavio offers the plug and gain approach for Source to Pay, providing an execution and variant analysis as well as best run scores . With the help of best run scores, you can understand different integration variants across SAP Ariba and SAP ERP and identify the best and worst practices in your organization.

SAP Signavio facilitates the establishment of a single source of process truth, decreases inefficient variants, and fosters a common understanding across organizational units, driving improved adoption for ERP and Ariba solutions through a data-driven approach to change management.

 

PRODUCT HIGHLIGHTS

Plug and gain approach: Execution Analysis, Process Variant Analysis, and Best Run Scores

  • Quickly identify inefficiencies and their effect on overall procurement lead times, such as delays in supplier deliveries
  • Understand cycle times across your whole Source to Pay process
  • Efficiently harmonize your process by targeting the most relevant variants for improvement, e.g., by selecting a specific supplier or specific material group

 

III. Build a Resilient Future

In an ever-evolving business landscape, building a resilient future demands a proactive approach to driving change.

SAP Signavio Process Insights equips organizations with the tools to accelerate the transition from the current to the desired future state to collaboratively and governely model the to-be process. With the quick modeler, you can easily model your as-is and to-be Source to Pay processes, share and view them, track changes, and manage versions efficiently.

Consider pre-defined process models as a starting point towards your own next procurement operating model, ranging from SAP Ariba Guided Buying, Sourcing to Contracts and Network. This empowers organizations to ensure compliance, decrease the impact of risks, such as disruptions in the supply chain, and set the stage for a resilient future.

 

PRODUCT HIGHLIGHTS

Pre-defined process models

  • Get inspiration into possible ways of running your business
  • Save time as skip the first steps of defining your to-be Source to Pay processes
  • Adjust the processes to your own needs

These process models are not part of the plug and gain approach.

 

 SAP Signavio empowers organizations to perform cross-system analysis at speed, leveraging pre-built content through direct access to the SAP Ariba system. SAP Signavio serves as a catalyst for procurement transformation by providing a robust suite of tools and capabilities to address critical challenges, optimize processes, and drive sustainable value across the organization. With its comprehensive features and proven results, SAP Signavio empowers organizations to lead the way toward procurement excellence.

With each release, we’re extending our capability set. Let’s explore our latest release on plug and gain for SAP Ariba in the next blog posts, with a very tangible exemplary customer story shared by our colleagues Katharina Minninger and @nklimenko.

entry.title: Re: bgRFC units in Transportation Management

entry.title: Salesforce Adapter download option not found

to download the Salesforce Adapter from this link  https://api.sap.com/package/SalesforceAdapter/overview .

Thanks,

Hira

entry.title: Re: SSO between Success Factors and Azure through IAS
entry.content:
Hi @Balaji1 ,

My customer wants to setup Multifactor Authentication using MS Azure (Entra).

  1. With the setup mentioned above, how can they enable MFA?
  2. Will the MFA be enabled in IAS/IPS or MS Azure?
  3. When activating SF Mobile App, how the MFA will be used? Is it every time when the user open SF Mobile app, the user to have authenticate using MS Authenticator or will it be just one time authentication?

entry.title: Unable to Open to Open Item List in SAP Business One 10
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have a problem in SAP Business One 10 (FP2208), HANA Db,
Some of our users are unable to open the Open Item List from the Sales - AR,

As per SAP note 3287549, they need to open the Open Item List from Inventory section.

Error "Table not found '' (Y) (ODBC - 2004) [Message 131-183]".

Since they are finance/Sales users, I cannot allow them to use the inventory section.


 

Can you guide me how can I solve this problem ?

Regards,

Salman

entry.title: Re: SAP Dynamic Forms 1.0 Released Features
entry.content:
Hi William,

Love to see this functionality coming. It will be of great value for a lot of processes.

The link to the product roadmap links to a sharepoint site with restricted access. Just looked at the SAP roadmap explorer of SAP but could not find it yet, also in the SSAM roadmap no further information is available. Could you provide the link to the roadmap without access restrictions?

Best 

Taekele Bijlsma

entry.title: Kitting Process in SAP Digital Manufacturing
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have gone through this video-WIP Video to setup WIP in our Tenant. But upon checking on SAP DM Roadmap, WIP linking with EWM is upcoming and not setup yet. 

*Requirement*: I am trying to setup kitting process in SAP DM for Discrete Industry. Is there any way to achieve this in DM out of the box?

Also, If someone please point me the exact use case for WIP. Is there any resource available where I can see the detailed use case for this. Any help will be valuable.

Thanks & Regards,
Kartik.

 

@ManoelCosta @Jonas_Haas @rahul_kumar_04 

SAP Digital Manufacturing SAP Digital Manufacturing Insights

entry.title: Re: SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting- Anonymize Candidate Profile and Applications
entry.content:
@Stefan_DEKA 
I am not sure if you really need anonymization for that. It sounds to me that your problem is easily solvable by using permissions.
Under "Manage Templates" you can configure permission for either the Candidate Profile or Candidate Application. There you can set view permissions on fields for roles and if I'm not mistaken even for certain steps in the pipeline (Application only I think)

Though you do need to configure the roles correctly (Recruiter - Hiring Manger and so on)

Hope this helps

entry.title: FM module to get all month-year for given date
entry.content:
HI All,

I need a FM to get all the months in mm.yyyy format. For example if I give input as 03.07.2024 to 03.07.2024 then output should be:

jun2024

jul2024

aug2024

sep2024

oct2024

nov2024

dec2024

jan2025

feb2025

.......upto  june2025.

Kindly let me know if any FM is available or else any other method to achieve this.

entry.title: Re: SAPUI5 SmartFilterBar setFilterData Issue
entry.content:
Hello

filter.setFilterDataAsString({"CompanyName":"sap"});

After setting the filter value to the Smart Filter then call rebindTable function to the smart table to load the data.
Thanks

entry.title: S4HC Warehouse Inventory Report for a single item received in 3 HU's - Reg
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

The business receives a single product in 3 different carton boxes due to its size Ex: Dressing Table where the doors / glass / frame will be placed separately. The expectation is to see the inventory in the warehouse as the stock is available in 3 carton boxes (HU's) though it is one single material. Do we have a standard solution available in SAP S4HANA Cloud?

Best Regards

SenthilNathan Karunanithi

entry.title: 'Edit' and 'Send email' buttons are not visible in standard fiori app 'Manage customer contacts'
entry.content:
I have added standard fiori app 'Manage customer contacts' to custom catalog. Checked all the services and its activated and roles have been assigned aswell.

However, I'm unable to see 'edit' and 'send email' buttons on top of the object page. In the same wayI have added 'Manage resuiisions app to the custom catalog but for that app ' Edit' and 'send email' buttons are visible.

Could anyone help on this ?. Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: Access Columns in SAC
entry.content:
The person who created the story can give access to others by names or by teams (or groups).

To give access, go to the file's location and click on the share icon (highlighted in red in the enclosed jpeg).

You can edit data in columns only in Planning Models, not in Analytical Models. The table below gives more details about access (in the enclosed jpeg).

entry.title: Re: Retain samples
Re: Retain samples
entry.content: Hi Enda, now 2 years later we also need this functionality for our customer and it still does not exist Do you have a solution how we can do that in combination to EWM?.

entry.title: Re: F-32 dump
entry.content:
Hello @imarco62 

See the note 2399366 - Short dump DYNPRO_NOT_FOUND in FB1D - you need to regenerate the screens with the RFFAKP01 report.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: datasphere exception - 2112401: Hostname 'www.googleapis.com;location' contains invalid characte
entry.content:
Hello Kevin, 

the note got updated and a solution is provided. But the solution does not solve the problem unfortunately. I updated my incident regarding this issue and I would recommend you to do the same. It seems that SAP support is not aware of the root cause of this problem.

Kind regards

Sven

Do anyone know if it is possible in S4/HANA Public Cloud to automatically create a physical inventory document (this material needs to be counted) in "Physical Inventory Monitoring" when a stock goes below a certain level?

entry.title: Re: BusinessObjects 4.3 SP4 - Patch 2/3/4/5 Issues
entry.content: We have encountered numerous #TOREFRESH errors on reports that have a hierarchy object as part of the report block. These reports work fine in our production system which is 4.3 SP3 Patch 3. Implemented SP4 on our sandbox client to fix auto-refresh issue and hit these #TOREFRESH issues. Installed SP4 patch 3 and then patch 4 (which was supposed to resolve this issue) and issues still exist. These are Webi docs built off a BW query datasource.

entry.title: Re: SAP Inside Track Brisbane July 2024
entry.content: The first list of questions/topics for the events are available on our site July 2024 | SAP Inside Track Brisbane (sitbne.github.io). Please make sure to submit your questions or topics on the "Future State of SAP Development" now....

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud Custom Widget Calls a SAP CPI RES...
entry.content:
Hi Chris,
This is the expected behavior, and the reason is that SAP Cloud Integration does not reply correctly to the HTTP OPTIONS call. You must use API Management before your SAP Cloud Integration endpoint to avoid CORS issues. 

Please find the tutorial as part of the SAP Learning" https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/btp-integration-suite-integral-api-management.html

The important thing is that your policy sets the correct response to every CORS request.

I hope this helps you.

Best regards 🖖🏻

entry.title: Handle multiple API Keys in SAP Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hi,

We are integrating with an external application, which has provided us company specific API key to post data using their API. For Ex : If we are posting data about Company A there is a specific API Key A. If Company B then API Key B. 

I can think of doing this using Value mapping but I would like to know if there is a better approach which is secure as well.  Please suggest

Best Regards

entry.title: Re: Beginner Level Understanding on SAP BTP Architecture
entry.content:
HI@RajChauhan 

  1. Yes. Rise customers rely on this diagram heavily.
  2. You can use DPAgent to do the replication or Cloud Connector.
  3. Private link is another way if you prefer all under private network. SAP Discovery Center Service - Private Link Service (cloud.sap)

entry.title: Re: MIGO Exit
entry.content:
Hello @Byz1 

You can use either user-exit MBCF0002 or MB_MIGO_ITEM_BADI enhancement.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: SAP AI Launchpad - Object Store Secret Connection
entry.content:
Are you trying to write a model?
Models are always written to the default secret, i.e. you need a secret with name "default".

entry.title: Changing to PBET enforces me using Posting Date
entry.content:
Dear experts,

When I try changing the Payment from FY-Based to Period-Based (PB-Encumbrance Tracking), I get error message, that I have to use Posting Date, instead of my DUE DATE definition.

Why is it enforce me to do so? I want to keep Due Date, and start using PBET.

 

Error Message: FI_E008

Using Update Profile 000200

 

Thanks,

Noy.

entry.title: Re: Service Order with Advance Shipment of Spare Parts
entry.content:
hi Girish, 

I propose to use here a consignment return process

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_BYDESIGN/2754875d2d2a403f95e58a41a9c7d6de/7f4e7f3e2d4349e4a6a39e2043e302f0.html?&version=2302

Maintain copy control from the sales order and sales order item which is used to deliver to consignment to the consignment return order type and item category. Then create consignment return order with reference to sales order item copy the product enter the quantity which should be returned.

This will not updated to the service order but at the end the parts are back in the normal storage location.

The return of the dismantled motor can be done with normal return or ARM process. With release 2023 FPS2, we are able to enter Repair Objects in the service order and then trigger In-house Repair and ARM. This could be a useful process if the dismantled motor needs to be repaired.

Regards, Gert

entry.title: Re: Service Order with Advance Shipment of Spare Parts
entry.content:
hi Fernando, 

Assigning service transaction types to sales order transaction types is possible, but optional. If it is not maintained, then the sales order type must just be equal to service order type (SRVO->SRVO by default)

Regards, Gert

entry.title: Re: Create external document (URL) via RFC using BDS_BUSINESSDOCUMENT_CRE_O_URL
entry.content: Hi i got the exactly same issue as you did, do you have any solution now?

entry.title: Re: Deletion of profit center
entry.content: Thank you. Your method has worked perfectly.

entry.title: Re: Message no. KD256:There are no accrued amounts; settl...
entry.content:
In my case, this error occurred when confirmation of production order happened in a month different from the one production order was created. Solution is to make settlement in the month when confirmation happened.

If the default posting date is the end of the new month, go to extras on KO88 and set your desired date.

entry.title: Re: In SAP S4Hana Public cloud - For Switzerland VAT report - 415 (input tax correction)

Hi,

I have included the tag SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Finance, as your question seems to be more related to Finance than SAP Central Business Configuration.

I hope this helps to categorize your post better and get answers in shorter period of time.

 

Best regards,

Tamas

entry.title: BC Set for CPM in S/4 HANA 2023 - Activation Issue
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Please note that we have activated all BC sets for CPM as per the Admin guide (From SFW_BROWSER) after activating all business functions.

But unfortunately system is not showing any configurations values based on the BC sets.

We are using S/4HANA 2023 FPS01.

Kindly advise.

Thanks

Abdul

entry.title: Full screen list in Maintenance Views
entry.content:
Introduction

Hi and welcome to my next SAP Community blog post.

With or without Fiori in the picture, Maintenance View is one of the main tools for maintaining SAP configuration. Reliable and easy to use, it is a proverbial workhorse.

However, since widescreen monitors became a standard, it has always been frustrating to me to see how much space is left unused (not to say wasted) in the list of a Maintenance View. The obvious solution was to modify the screen manually, but this was overwritten whenever a list was recreated.

Today, I will show you a way of making the Maintenance View list use as much space as possible, always.

Implementation

To achieve this, a modification has to be done to a standard SAP include responsible for creating the screen. The include is called MSVIMF21.

The list screen is created by a function call RPY_DYNPRO_INSERT in two places - line 167 and 226.

To have a list as wide as possible, the calls' parameters have to be modified - a new screen and list size has to be provided and buttons moved (to make the room for the bigger list). This can be achieved by modifying following parameters:

  • HEADER - here a new maximum size for the screen has to be provided
     
  • FLDS_TO_CONT - here a new position for the Maintenance View list buttons has to be provided
    • For NAME = VIM_POSI_PUSH
    • For NAME = VIM_POSITION_INFO
  • CONT - last but not least, the new size for the table with the records
     

After these changes are done, the new screen will be generated with a maximum size allowed and with resizing options checked (these are checked by default) - which makes the screen to be sized down in case of a lower resolution.

Summary

Thanks to this modification, a list screen of all Maintenance Views will use as much space as possible, making it easier to view and/or maintain configuration. The difference between the standard size and the modified size is clearly visible.

Standard:

Modified:

entry.title: 回复： Watch the SAP BW Modernization Webinar Series
entry.content:
👍Great contents！

Could you please upload PDF for Unit 3:Lesson 4

entry.title: Re: How to connect SAP CPI-PI and SAP Data intelligence
entry.content: @Bhargavakrishna Hello Is it possible to connect from SAP DI to SAP CPI using CPI-PO operator? or CPI can use https sender(BW4System and TokenService) to consume data from BW4HANA ADSO directly? Please throw some heavy light since this topic is too non transparent. Flow: BW4HANA -> SAP DI -> SAP CPI -> S4HANA /ECC

entry.title: Re: Public cloud assign shipping data for storage locatio...
entry.content:
Hello,

 

You should be able to create shipping points in CBC in "Set Up Organizational Structure" application, please check below note:

3296958 - Unable to Create/Add the Organizational entities using respective SSCUI's (Self-Service-Configurations-UI) in CBC

Additionally, you can check our SAP Help link below:

Setting Up Organizational Structure

"Under the company code, you can create Sales Organization and Plant, depending on the scope you have chosen earlier. Also you can create multi-assignment units (for example, shipping points and divisions) as needed by choosing Create button."

 

I hope this helps to fulfill your requirement, in case of further questions you can update the thread.

 

Best regards,

Tamas

entry.title: Re: House Keeping activities in a non cumulative ADSO (In...
entry.content:
Hi Oscar

 

This is something available in BW/4HANA 2021:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BW4HANA/107a6e8a38b74ede94c833ca3b7b6f51/321a4042ffba4898a68003116192d775.html?locale=en-US&version=2021.09

It is possible to delete:

  • Reference points
  • Use date as filter criteria for the deletion (only <= supported)

br

Roland

entry.title: Re: datasphere exception - 2112401: Hostname 'www.googleapis.com;location' contains invalid characte
entry.content:
Thanks Sven for your heads up.

I also noticed the update in notes and I followed a testing with their proposal to update the ODBC driver version. but unfortunately, the same, it doesn't work for remote table in my side as well. still with the same error message and connection broken.

I didn't raise an incident yet, since we are doing a more internal product, no customer specified.

entry.title: Re: Logic behind Financial roll-up from PS to PPM
entry.content:
Thanks Ken for your response. 

But, I don't think there is any setting/parameters SAP offer to make/reset the financial value '0' and restore back the same or updated values in the next run.

Yes, we have a wrapper Z program (ZSCHEDULE_PPM_FIN_ROLLUP) to schedule the job for running the standard roll-up program /RPM/FICO_INT_PLANNING.

That wrapper program is a customized solution built by SAP itself around 2-3 years ago to improve the performance. Looks like, the same wrapper program is offering by SAP now via an SAP note 3390324 - Wrapper reports for Financial Integration & Planning scenarios as a standard functionality.

Does that wrapper program is causing that behaviour (value to zero and restore back to value)?

Please suggest.

entry.title: Aging report for all customers or suppliers instead of top ten
entry.content:
For aging reports "Overdue Receivable" and "Overdue Payables" app, it only has the option to display the top 10 customers' or suppliers' data.
I want to display the report with all customers and suppliers.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Aging report for all customers or suppliers instead o...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

You can kindly refer to the KBA below to create your own report based on the standard app.
KBA 3129588 - Accounts receivable aging report for all customers instead of top 10 customers.
https://me.sap.com/notes/3129588

Thanks & Best regards

entry.title: Re: EC UI is displaying the Option ID from a picklist ins...

entry.title: Re: Submissions for "SAP HANA Cloud multi-model" developer challenge: Week 2 - Words as Ve
entry.content:
Hello, 

I have little confusion. I have completed till Step 6.

But I am not able to understand from step 7 as below.

  1. Open and execute week2* notebooks from the challenges folder

Do I need to execute this in step 7 ?

Regards,

Debopriya Ghosh

entry.title: Re: Meet your new friend EMIS: Event Mesh in SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Thanks for the insights. Sounds like existing SAP Event Mesh might converge into EMIS in the future? Could you say something about that?

Also some sort of rough tabular comparison of SAP Event Mesh, EMIS and SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh might come in handy 🙂

Cheers

Jens

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I feel really delighted to join this community and look forward to learning from and contributing to the community.

Have a great day!

entry.title: SAP “情况处理” (Situation Handling)
entry.content:
当用户没有注意到一些关键业务情况，比如需要待处理的确认等，则有可能对正常业务产生负面影响。“情况处理(Situation Handling)”是一个针对异常情况进行提醒和通知的功能。通过设置，可以在预定范围内选择哪些业务在哪些异常情况下将提醒发给哪些用户。这些用户可以：

  • 在SAP Fiori登录界面查看异常情况通知
  • 在相关SAP Fiori app内查看异常情况信息
  • 使用My Situations应用查看异常情况信息
  • 从异常情况导航到相关的业务应用，以供用户详细查看情况并做相应处理

以下我们来看一下在SAP S/4HANA公有云情况处理这一功能的实现过程。

首先使用Manage Teams and Responsibilities (F2412) app创建团队，维护接收异常情况的团队和团队成员：

系统中已有预定义好的情况类型模板，我们也可以通用app Manage Situation Types (F2947)来创建新的情况类型。如关于MRP的情况类型模板SAP_PP_MRP_MATERIAL_EXCEPTION是用来监控物料是否有需要取消的MRP元素，有的话就发出通知消息给用户。我们拷贝该情况类型模板来创建一个新的情况类型：

填写应用内显示情况消息的文本描述和导航地址：

显示情况消息的数据条件：

接收情况消息的团队和责任人：

设置好后可以直接触发作业来创建情况实例：

点击创建和启用，完成情况类型的设置。

当有物料存在应取消的MRP元素时，比如以下截图中多余的生产订单，团队用户就可以收到相关的异常情况消息了。

可以在SAP Fiori登录页面看到消息：

也可以在app My Situations里面查看所有的situation message

在信situation message中还可以导航到预设定的应用页面：

这个地址是在情况类型中进行设置的：

————————————————————————————————————————————
**本博文中涉及到的截图均来源于SAP内部测试环境。

entry.title: Re: Important Update: Changes to the Free Tier Option for SAP BTP ABAP Environment
entry.content:
Hi @benlim ,

 

you can use start/stop with a simple trick - schedule a system to stop in a recurrence every night, but not start up until some time in the future, such as 31.12.2999. In this scenario, the system would shut down automatically every night and you would start it up manually.

Best Regards,

Burcu

entry.title: SSAM2310 Measurment Documents full load in each delta sync
entry.content:
Hello,

we are working with SSAM2310 on customer side and are facing issues with the Measurement Documents in Delta Sync.

The sync is fast, but after we changed any data (confirmation, WorkOrder, Notification,...) and press sync --> all the Measurment Documents are loaded once again. We have many data in there, so this step costs us >20sec.

We did not change any settings in this OMDO. Does anybody know, how to configure that the Delta is not ignored or does anybody have the same problem?

Regards

Björn

entry.title: Getting Message no. F2803 error while using "FI_WT_VENDOR_UPDATE" functional module in SE37
entry.content: This is error I am getting while uploading standard bapi program "FI_WT_VENDOR_UPDATE" in SE37 TCode, System error in table LFBW Message no. F2803.

entry.title: Re: Reclassification in SAP Group reporting
entry.content: Hey, if I understand the question correctly, using one of the posting options in the Settings tab in Reclassification Rules might do the trick.

entry.title: Re: I am unable to submit data using planning in SAP BW v...
entry.content:
Hi Martin,

I really appreciate your reply.

I couldn't agree more with the method of using more than one info object to store data in a DSO.
Since the maximum characters allowed for reporting is only 250, the only way to handle this is by splitting the data into multiple info objects.

Thank you.

Regards,
Faisal

entry.title: Re: I am unable to submit data using planning in SAP BW v...
entry.content: Hi Martin, I really appreciate your reply. I couldn't agree more with the method of using more than one info object to store data in a DSO. Since the maximum characters allowed for reporting is only 250, the only way to handle this is by splitting the data into multiple info objects. Regards, Faisal

entry.title: Re: How can I clear a draft of a canceled document from t...
entry.content:
Dear @alplam ,

This workaround may be suitable for you.

Create a custom report for by employee wise pending document approval, therefore as part of HR protocol, the pending document has been to cleared.

In recent update, there is option to remove multiple drafts as well.

Thanks.

entry.title: Message no. F2803
entry.content: Getting this error while updating vendor withholding tax details in "FI_WT_VENDOR_UPDATE" BAPI in SE37.

entry.title: Re: Replication of Valid User name from SF EC to SAP S/4 or SAP ERP in case of Multiple Employments
entry.content:
Hi Deepika, 

I am sorry that I  could not get the time to check your message. Please let me know is your issue resolved or shall help any ?

entry.title: Need to enter measuring document in change maintenance order webdynpro app
entry.content:
I Have mapped a measuring point as running hours as a prt tool for a operation in the task list.

when order is created i have to enter measuring document in order itself. is there any way in standard.

because it is appearing only during the confirmation stage.

 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Goods Return without GRPO
entry.content:
Dear @ali93 ,

You should open the Goods Return and use Copy From to choose the respective Goods Receipt PO to process the return. You must know against which Goods Receipt the quantities is being returned, so the vendor can also account them.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Looking for an S4 Odata V2 contains below Shipment fi...
entry.content: Hi @EszterN , Thanks for the update. Sure, I can use V2 but can you suggest any standard Odata V2 which contains above fields. If standard OData is not available, I will go with a custom build.

entry.title: Re: Reclassification in SAP Group reporting
entry.content:
Sorry -- I hit post too soon. 
In the Settings tab -> Document Settings -> Post to partner unit, you have these options.

By choosing "Post to any unit", you get to select the unit on the source/destination page:

Hope this helps.

Rgds Trond

entry.title: Re: The value of the 'Tax' output parameter of the formul...
entry.content:
Dear @CarlosSantos ,

The tax formula is incorrect. You obtain the knowledge of using the tax formula, please refer the default demo database, the examples available there, will give you an idea on how to use the tax parameters in the tax formula.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: datasphere exception - 2112401: Hostname 'www.googleapis.com;location' contains invalid characte
entry.content:
Hello, 

@kevin_yang @Sven_Knöpfler 

We opened an incident about REMOTE TABLES and GBQ, and the solution proposed is to update de GBQ Driver???

To me this answer is very surprising.

As far as I know, and as reflected in the SAP documentation, the ODBC driver is a requirement for Data Flows (specifically when we use a GBQ object directly in a DF without importing it as a remote table in space).
But the ODBC driver is not used in remote tables.

In our case, all connections to GBQ that do not have the location specified work fine, but those that do do not. Even if you delete the location or try to create a new connection, it fails.

Honestly we have been unable to provide important data to business users since Sunday, and the proposed solution only makes me more worried.

entry.title: How to download configurations from my CBC project
entry.content:
Hello,

 

Is it possible to download my configuration from SAP Central Business Configuration project?

If yes, how to use the feature?

 

Best regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to download configurations from my CBC project
entry.content:
Hello,

 

There is a possibility to download a zip file containing information on configuration data and selected scope of your project.

This function is highly efficient in case there is a requirement for example to copy current configuration and scope into a new project.

 

There are certain prerequisites to using this function, please see below steps:

 

       1. Make sure you have necessary role assigned

In order to Download Template Configuration, you need to have the role "Content Processor" assigned to your user.

The business role Content Processor (SAP_CBC_CONTENT_PROCESSOR) was delivered with 2402 version in the standard software update.

If the system was provisioned in a previous release the role will not be available by default in your IAS system.

More information on how to get this role into your system in case it is missing can be found in below SAP KBA:

3453205 - "Prepare Download of My Configuration" button is not available in CBC Project

More information on Standard Roles & Authorization in CBC can be found at SAP Help - Authorization link.

 

       2. Your CBC project must be in the correct phase

Downloading Template Configuration is only possible in Product-Specific Configuration phase.

In case your project is still in Scope and Organizational Structure phase, you need to complete all activities and move your project to the next phase.

Please see SAP Help - Completing the Scope and Organizational Structure Phase

 

 

What data will be downloaded?

 

Summary file:

  • A list of the configuration adaptations that you have maintained.
  • A list of the selected scope.

Individual files for each configuration activity with the settings you have made.

In case you are using a 2-System Landscape the downloaded file contains the settings made in the quality system.

 

 

Downloading Procedure step by step:

  1. As a first step to download the configuration data of your project, go to the Project Details section and choose Prepare Download of My Configuration.
    This prepares the download. You need to wait until the button changes to Download My Configuration.
  2. Choose Download My Configuration.
  3. Enter a name for your file and choose SaveDownloads folder.
  4. Once the zip file download is complete, Download My Configuration changes back to Prepare Download of My Configuration and you can perform the action again.

The download will be logged in the Change Logs section

(Please note that if you use the Apple Safari browser, refresh the browser manually after the download of the zip file has been done to display Prepare Download of My Configuration again.)

 

Summary:

Downloading Template Configuration feature is used to:

  • Extract selected scope from your project
  • Extract the configurations maintained in your project
  • Extract any adaptations executed in the S/4HANA Cloud target system via SSCUI's

Note: This data can only be downloaded in excel format.

Further information on this feature can be found on at:

 SAP Help - Downloading Template Configuration

 Downloading Your Business Configuration Adaptations in SAP Central Business Configuration

entry.title: Re: The value of the 'Tax' output parameter of the formul...
entry.content:
Hi @CarlosSantos ,

Looks like you have a circular reference in your tax combination.

Your ICMS has IPI in its BC, which means you must have IPI first in your combination (without IPI tax amount calculated, you cannot pass it to ICMS), but, at the same time, your IPI has ICMS in the BC, meaning you'll need ICMS calculated first, which obviously would end up failing considering the whole logic.

I suggest you create an external "memoria de calculo" using Excel/Google Sheets and after reaching the correct base amounts, rates and tax amounts per tax type, you may transport it to SAP or even share it here, then we can solve this together.

Best regards,
Felipe

entry.title: Re: Fiori List Report displays identical records despite...
entry.content:
The problem is the primary keys. They have to be unique for every row that is returned.

This should fix the problem.

entry.title: Metal bar management
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I have a problem that I don't know how to solve in SAP B1 regarding the management of iron bars in the warehouse. Suppose I have 10 whole bars of 6 m each in the warehouse for a certain item. The unit of measure I currently use for the warehouse is the number of pieces, so in SAP I see 10. For a certain job, I use 3 m of a bar, so in the production order, I indicate the use of half a bar, and after closing the production order, the warehouse shows 9.5. Now, I open a new job for which a 4.5 m piece of bar (0.75 of a bar) is needed. How can I tell SAP to consider using a new whole bar and not simply do the subtraction 9.5-0.75? Is it possible to manage the warehouse so that SAP considers both whole bars and pieces? Thank you to anyone who can help me.

entry.title: Re: Unable to Open to Open Item List in SAP Business One 10
entry.content:
Hi,

There are 2 options

  1. Open Item List report appears in many modules: sales, purchasing, inventory, production. The workaround from SAP Note is not related to authorization. It's just other door to access Open Item List without error. Just guide your user to use the report in Inventory Module instead of Sales Module.
  2. Upgrade your system to Ver 10 FP 2305 to fix the issue.

Hope this helps,

Son Tran

entry.title: Re: datasphere exception - 2112401: Hostname 'www.googleapis.com;location' contains invalid characte
entry.content:
Thanks @XaviPolo . I was also "surprised" by this answer because of the reasons you mentioned.

Probably not an coincidence: the SAP HANA Cloud was upgraded to 2024.14 on the last weekend, exactly since then the GBQ connection is not working anymore.

entry.title: How to Analyze Unexpected Runtime Behavior of The CDS Annotations Values in Your ABAP Data Model?
entry.content:
Imagine, you’re asked to add a CDS annotation to a data definition. After doing

that, the value of the annotation is not reflected as expected in your ABAP data model (ADM). What to do next to analyze the runtime behavior in accordance? Do you know the relevant CDS tools in ABAP development tools for Eclipse (ADT) how to find this out? If not, lay bag and relax. Enjoy reading my blog.

Note: You can use CDS annotations for the whole CDS object, elements, fields, and parameters to add domain-specific metadata, such as text labels, for example object names, layer information, switch status, and so on, to your ADM.

For more information, see Annotations in ABAP CDS

Active Annotation View

The Active Annotations view displays the whole structure of a data definition with its elements, parameters, and fields in a tree. In addition, you can see here the according active values, texts, and the names of the contributing original resources column-wise.

This means, you get here a clear overview with all the relevant information that relates to your data definition with only a few clicks. That’s great, isn’t?

For more information, see Active Annotations View

Activation and Evaluation of CDS Annotations

When activating a CDS object, the values of the containing annotations will be saved in internal system tables. From here, they can be accessed and used for evaluation.

The evaluation of annotations is performed in a predefined order. This means, if there is a value for the same field several times provided in your ADM, the system will consider the relevant values in accordance. If the value is …

  1. handled by a metadata extension, it will be on top. This means, no other value of this field will then be considered.
  2. a direct annotation that is defined and handled in the actual data definition, this value will be considered when no value of the same field is provided by a metadata extension.
  3. a indirect annotation that is selected, derived, or inherited from another data sources, this value will be considered when the cases from step 1 and 2 don’t apply.

For more information, see

  • ABAP CDS - Evaluation of Annotations
  • ABAP CDS - Evaluation of Metadata Extensions

Sample

Here, the Active Annotation view has been opened for the CDS_View_Test_2 data definition. The structure with the annotations that are defined for the whole CDS object is followed by the entity annotations that are effective for the respective fields. In addition to the values, the additional metadata, such as the annotation value, label text, and so on, is displayed column-wise.

The active values are displayed in accordance to the order how the CDS annotations are evaluated. See above. Beside visualizing the ADM, you can also navigate, for example, to the origin data source from the displayed annotation value. To do this, just double-click the relevant name.

Note that you can switch between the flat and structured representation. For example, if you work with a huge ADM and you prefer a flat structure, don’t worry and just click the icon with the 3 dots in the integrated toolbar and choose Flat representation.

Use

You can trigger this view for …

  1. the complete data definition: Select the data definition in the Project Explorer or select the name of the CDS entity.
  2. the complete CDS entity: Select the name of the CDS entity in the ABAP CDS source code.
  3. a specific field or parameter: Select the relevant field or element in the ABAP CDS source code.

After your selection, choose Open With… > Active Annotation from the context menu.

Annotation Propagation View

The Annotation Propagation view visualizes from which CDS view, ABAP Dictionary object, or metadata extension the annotation values have been merged as well as which values have not been considered in a tabular way. This means, you get a list with all relating data sources and their metadata per row.

If there is a value for the annotation from a data source, this value will then be evaluated as I have described it above. The value which will be considered is coloured black otherwise in grey.

For more information, see

  • Annotation Propagation View
  • Propagation of Annotations

Sample

Here, the Annotation Propagation view has been triggered for field_4 that is used in the CDS_View_Test_2 data definition. The respective annotation value for this field is merged from the metadata extension CUSTOMER_EXTENSION_2 and the data definition CDS_VIEW_TEST_1. The values from higher levels are marked in black. This means, these values are active. The value from the metadata extension PARTNER_EXTENSION_2 is marked in grey. This means, this value is inactive and won’t be considered.

You can easily adapt your selection and update the content to be displayed. In the Annotations For input field choose Browse… and add the relevant new basis.

In addition, you can also navigate to the displayed data source. To do this, just double-click the name of the relevant object.

Use

You open this view on the same way how the Active Annotation view. Open the context menu from the relevant position and choose Open With… > Annotation Propagation from the context menu. The Annotation Propagation view will then be opened in a new view below the editor.

Data Preview

I’m sure you already know the data preview. It shows you the values of the data records that are retrieved from your ADM on a tabular way. This helps you to get a clear overview about the values that are selected from your CDS view quickly.

For more information, see Data Preview

Sample

Here, the data preview has been opened for the R_TRAVEL_D data definition. It shows the data and values that are retrieved with your ADM.

Use

You have the following possibilities to open the data preview from the context menu.

The Data Preview… option with the 3 dots allows you to limit the number of fields to be displayed.

This limits the number of columns and makes it more reader-friendly for you. You can change filter whenever you need it. Just select or deselect the relevant entries.

In addition, you can trigger filtering also from the integrated toolbar:

In a new tab, the filter field is then opened.

Here, you can start typing your search string.

Note that you might need to consider lower and upper case. To bring your search input into the relevant style, you can use the following shortcuts:

Shortcut

Description

Ctrl+Shift+X

Converting entries in the input field to upper case

Ctrl+Shift+Y

Converting entries in the input field to lower case

Features

When selecting an entry in the data preview, you have the following possibilities to trigger certain functionalities from the context menu:

From the context menu, you can choose:

  • Quick Filter On
  • Distinct Values For
  • Follow Association
  • Copy / Copy All Rows As

Quick Filter On

You can filter the data preview for a specific value very easily. Select the relevant value and choose Quick Filter On from the context menu.

Then, only the lines with the respective values are displayed.

Distinct Values For

Choose this function if you want to find the duplicate values that are merged by your CDS view. Click the relevant value in the table and choose Distinct Values For in the context menu.

You will then get an overview how often a value is duplicate in your ADM.

This list helps you investigate duplicates in your ADM. In addition, you can use it to estimate if the ADM selects the expected data.

Following Associations

In the data preview, you can toggle through the association order of a value. This means, you can check from which data source the associated value is merged from. ADT will bring you to your target.

After selection, the list of associations is displayed in a dialog.

From here you can navigate to the relevant association. If you want to get back to the original data preview, use the breadcrumb on top of the Data Preview view.

Copy Features

Copy

You can copy the content of a cell, row, or the selected column.

 

Copy All Rows As

You can copy and export all displayed row as ABAP value statement or CSV file.

Feedback & Discussion

Was this content helpful for you? Do you have further question? Just leave a comment below and let’s have a discussion! I look forward to hearing from you!

entry.title: Re: Old Locks on table FDC_TIMERDAEMON_LOCK in SM12
entry.content: What if the clean up process never stops? Is it okay to see it's running for very long periods like days, weeks or even month?

entry.title: Transportation Fuel cost: Deduct charges from freight cost when FSD is Posted
entry.content:
*Requirement* :

The business issues fuel (diesel) to carriers from their own petrol pumps. When the carrier submits the invoice, the business wants to deduct the amount for the fuel issued while settling the invoice with the carrier.

 *Note* :

1. The carrier invoice will not include the amount of fuel issued; the carrier will present the actual transportation cost and any additional charges, if applicable.

2. When MIRO is posted with reference to the freight order, the business wants to see a split of fuel cost and actual transportation cost.

3. The fuel cost and actual transportation cost should be recorded against two different G/L accounts.

 *The following options are not working:* 

(i) Configuring fuel charges as a negative charge in the TM side restricts the visibility of fuel cost deductions in the PO and SES. 

(ii) The credit memo concept is also not working.

Please help if you have encountered such requirements in your projects.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: SAP Dynamic Forms 1.0 Released Features
entry.content: it reminds me the SAP Interactive Forms by Adobe. Is this new BTP service the successor ? Is it plan to deliver migration utilities to switch to this new solution ?

entry.title: Re: Validate if the swift/bic field is empty through a BADI in tx fi02
entry.content: There is a customer-exit EXIT_SAPLBANK_001 (SMOD SAPLBANK) called at start of module PRUEFEN to add your own check.

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM: requirement approval with external workflow management system
entry.content:
Objectives

You want to externalize SAP Cloud ALM requirement approval to external workflow management systems like SAP Business Process Automation.

 

Prerequisites

Any workflow management external system can be connected to SAP Cloud ALM using the provided External API Integration capabilities.
The external tool must:

  • Expose REST APIs to receive messages.
  • Expose an API endpoint accessible from your SAP Cloud ALM BTP sub-account.
  • Support one of the following authentication protocol described in the following documentation.
  • Support a compatible payload for REST operations. The list of supported mapping rules can be found in the help.sap.com.

If the external tools do not match the requirements for a simple integration, an integration can still be achieved by using SAP Integration Suite or a third-party API Integration HUB platform.

 

Procedure Overview

  • Configure a workflow system to manage the requirement approval.
  • Configure an SAP BTP destination to enable connectivity to your workflow system.
  • Configure an SAP Cloud ALM web-hook to enable communication between your external system and your SAP Cloud ALM tenant.
  • Use the built-in SAP Cloud ALM mapping “Workflow to SAP Business Process Automation Workflow” to define the transformation rules to apply to messages exchanges between SAP Cloud ALM and the external REST API.
  • Add a SAP Cloud ALM subscription to associate the external system with the event types raised by SAP Cloud ALM.
  • Configure your SAP Cloud ALM Project to associate SAP Cloud ALM subscriptions to the requirement workflow type.

 

Configure a workflow system

In this example, the workflow system used is SAP Build Process Automation.

A basic process is defined:

Following parameters can be sent by SAP Cloud ALM:

You can download this SAP build process example here: Approval Workflow 2 eyes_1.0.2.mtar and adjust it to your needs.

Check the help.sap.portal for more information.

 

Configure SAP BTP destination

In your SAP BTP Cockpit create a destination to your external Azure Dev Ops system. The SAP BTP destination enables the connectivity from your SAP BTP tenant to your external system.

 

Configure SAP Cloud ALM Web-hook

In the SAP Cloud ALM External API Management application, create a web-hook for your destination with the following parameters:

  • External resource type: Task. 
  • Destination type: Enter Other.

Check the help.sap.portal for more information.

 

Create a SAP Cloud ALM mapping

The mapping defines the transformation rule to apply before sending the request and after receiving the response from the external system.

Copy the built-in “Workflow to SAP Business Process Automation Workflow” mapping to adjust it to your needs. In below example, priority and scope are added to the original mapping.

The mapping content is:

 

 

{
  "_information": {
    "_version": "1",
    "_source": "SAP Cloud ALM Workflow",
    "_destination": "SAP Build",
    "_name": "SAP Cloud ALM Task to SAP Build",
    "_comment": ""
  },
  "switches": [
    {
      "name": "switchLink",
      "cases": [
        {
          "when": "CALMREQU",
          "then": "ptmRequirement-management?sap-app-origin-hint=&tile=PTMREQ&"
        },
        {
          "when": "CALMDEF",
          "then": "ptmDefect-management?sap-app-origin-hint=&tile=PTMDEF&"
        },
        {
          "when": "CALMUS",
          "then": "task-management?sap-ui-app-id-hint=com.sap.calm.imp.tkm.ui&"
        },
        {
          "when": "CALMTASK",
          "then": "task-management?sap-ui-app-id-hint=com.sap.calm.imp.tkm.ui&"
        }
      ],
      "default": "task-management?sap-ui-app-id-hint=com.sap.calm.imp.tkm.ui&"
    },
    {
      "name": "switchExternalStatus",
      "cases": [
        {
          "when": "RUNNING",
          "then": "STARTED"
        },
        {
          "when": "CANCELED",
          "then": "FAILED"
        },
        {
          "when": "ERRONEOUS",
          "then": "FAILED"
        },
        {
          "when": "COMPLETED",
          "then": "STARTED"
        },
        {
          "when": "SUSPENDED",
          "then": "STARTED"
        }
      ],
      "default": "FAILED"
    },
    {
      "name": "switchPriority",
      "cases": [
        {
          "when": 10,
          "then": "Highest"
        },
        {
          "when": 20,
          "then": "High"
        },
        {
          "when": 30,
          "then": "Medium"
        },
        {
          "when": 40,
          "then": "Low"
        },
        {
          "when": 40,
          "then": "Lowest"
        }
      ],
      "default": "Lowest"
    }
  ],
  "structure": {
    "request": {
      "definitionId": "#{@USER_VAR(workflowDefinitionId)}",
      "context": {
        "subject": "#{item.title}",
        "desc": "#{item.description}",
        "status": "#{item.status}",
        "id": "#{item.id}",
        "link": "#{@CLOUD_ALM_URL}/launchpad#${SWITCH(switchLink,item.type)}/taskDetail/#{item.id}",
        "triggeredBy": "#{triggeredBy}",
        "projectId": "#{item.projectId}",
        "reviewedBy": "#{@USER_VAR(reviewedBy)}",
        "priority": "${SWITCH(switchPriority,item.priorityId)}",
        "scope": "#{item.scopeName}",
        "timestamp": "#{item.createdTimestamp}"
      }
    },
    "response": {
      "externalResourceId": "#{id}",
      "externalResourceStatus": "${SWITCH(switchExternalStatus,status)}"
    }
  },
  "eventTypeMapping": {
    "_comment": "Specific event type mapping",
    "operationMappings": [
      {
        "eventType": "WORKFLOW.STARTED",
        "operation": "POST",
        "responseRoot": "",
        "externalIdProperty": "/id"
      },
      {
        "eventType": "WORKFLOW.CANCELED",
        "operation": "PATCH",
        "responseRoot": "",
        "path": "/#{@CLOUD_ALM_EXTERNAL_ID}",
        "structure": {
          "request": {
            "status": "CANCELED",
            "cascade": false
          },
          "response": {
            "sentCancelRequest": true,
            "externalResourceStatus": "FAILED"
          }
        }
      }
    ]
  }
}

 

 

In this example, we use the following mapping details:

 

Mapping

Description

"eventType":"WORKFLOW.STARTED",

"operation":"POST",

"responseRoot":"",

"externalIdProperty":"/id"

The externalIdProperty property is defined in the response mapping of a creation event to store the ID of the workflow in the built-in variable @Cloud_ALM_EXTERNAL_ID.

The value of the externalIdProperty should follow the JSON pointer syntax.

"eventType":"WORKFLOW.CANCELED",

"operation":"PATCH",

"responseRoot":"",

"path":"/#{@CLOUD_ALM_EXTERNAL_ID}"

During a workflow cancelation, we pass the workflow ID as a path parameter with the built-in variable: @Cloud_ALM_EXTERNAL_ID.

"#{@USER_VAR(workflowDefinitionId)}"

The workflowDefinitionId property is defined in the subscription and is the ID to the SAP Build Process Automation workflow. You can find it in the SAP Build monitoring application.

Check the help.sap.portal for more information.

 

Add a SAP Cloud ALM subscription

Create a subscription based on your web-hook and your mapping with the following parameters:

  • Resource type: workflow to use this subscription for workflow events raised for resources managed by the project and task applications.
  • Type: Built-in to apply a transformation payload.

The workflowDefinitionId property is the ID to the SAP Build Process Automation workflow. You can find it in the SAP Build monitoring application.

Check the help.sap.portal for more information.

 

Configure your SAP Cloud ALM project

In the Subscriptions panel of the SAP Cloud ALM Project Management application, associate the subscription to the selected task types in the workflow column.

In this example, workflow events will be sent for Requirement. Other tasks types are not yet supported.
All workflow events (kick-off or cancelation) will be managed by the associated subscriptions.

Check the help.sap.portal for more information.

 

Monitor resources changes events

In the SAP Cloud ALM External API Management application, you can monitor the events exchanged with your external system.

 

Results

In the SAP Cloud ALM Requirements application, all requirements of projects with a workflow subscription will appear like this:

When the status is changed to “Ready for Approval”:

Then if the requirement is approved in the external workflow system:

Or rejected:

 

More Information

  • SAP Cloud ALM API Guide

Thanks for reading

entry.title: Re: Understanding Different Types of RFC Calls in SAP ABAP
entry.content:
For asynchronous RFC, don't use PERFORMING use CALLING

CALL FUNCTION 'ZFM_CREATE_ZBSP001_TAB'
  DESTINATION 'NONE'
  STARTING NEW TASK 'TASK1'
  CALLING return_code ON END OF TASK

Where return_code is a method.

Performs are obsolete and should not be used in new code. Nor in your examples.

entry.title: Re: Error while doing the AP invoice based on the GRPO -...
entry.content:
Hi,

Check for the same

the SAP Note 3115436 - Error Occurs When Copying GRPO to A/P Invoice - SAP for Me

Hope this helps,

Son Tran

entry.title: Re: How to get Tables used inside a t-code?
entry.content: This program only displays tables directly used by the report. If they call function modules that read from tables you won't know about those.

entry.title: Transport Access
entry.content:
I am doing and audit over SAP environment and i am having a confusion regarding access to release transports to production environment.

I understand to release transports to production environment it requires access to 

STMS_import with activity 60

however, I cannot understand what's the function of Activity 43.
